zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland於1296年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾凱爾時，他的父親James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland53歲，母親Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster33歲。他於1315年與Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland在蘇格蘭艾斯爾登東納爾德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1327年4月9日在蘇格蘭Linlithgowshire巴斯蓋特享年31歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland was born in 1296, in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, his father, James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland, was 53 and his mother, Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster, was 33. He married Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland in 1315, in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 9 April 1327, in Bathgate, Linlithgowshire, Scotland, at the age of 31, and was buried in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland est né en 1296, à Kyle, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland, avait 53 ans et sa mère, Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland en 1315, à Dundonald, Ayrshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 9 avril 1327, à Bathgate, Linlithgowshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 31 ans, et a été enterré à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland 1296 in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland 53 und seine Mutter Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster 33. Er hat 1315 in Dundonald, Ayrshire, Scotland Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 9. April 1327 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Bathgate, Linlithgowshire, Scotland und wurde in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland begraben.

ko.Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Sc: 영국 스코틀랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리 파이슬리 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland(이)가 1296년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 키일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland(은)는 53세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1315년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 던도널드에서 Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1327년 4월 9일 스컷랜드 Linlithgowshire 바스가트에서 31세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리에 묻혔다.

ru.Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Sc: пеисли эбби, пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter Stewart 6th High Steward of Scotland родился в 1296 г. в местности кил, аиршир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени James Stewart 5th High Steward of Scotland было 53 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Egidia de Burgh of Ulster, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Majorie Bruce Princess of Scotland в 1315 г. в местности дундональд, аиршир, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 9 апреля 1327 г. в местности батгэит, Linlithgowshire, Шотландия в возрасте 31 год и был похоронен в местности пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия.
